reports.title: Seagate: F3Q26 Review: Favorable Demand & Pricing Drive Beat & Raise to Eclipse High Expectations; Raise Estimates & PT
reports.headline: F3Q26 beat/raise on data center demand, favorable HDD pricing, HAMR mix, and opex discipline; JPM raises ests and Dec-26 PT to $775 from $600, keeps OW.
reports.summary: JPM reiterates OW on STX after a strong F3Q26 beat and better F4Q26 guide, driven by hyperscaler/data center demand, +5% q/q avg rev per EB, 70%+ y/y incremental GM, and rising HAMR/Mozaic mix. Mgmt raised MT rev growth target to at least 20% from low-to-mid-teens as Nearline EB capacity is largely allocated through CY27 and planning discussions extend into CY28+. JPM materially lifts EPS ests and PT, arguing the HDD cycle has shifted from commodity-like price declines to supply-constrained pricing and margin expansion.
important_observations: F3Q26 rev/EPS/GM all beat JPM and cons; rev $3.112bn vs cons $2.954bn, GM 47.0% vs cons 44.6%, EPS $4.10 vs cons $3.50.
Pricing was a key variant datapoint: avg rev per EB rose +5% q/q despite typical HDD industry price declines.
HAMR mix estimated at 30%+ of Nearline EB shipments in F3Q26, exiting FY26 at ~40%, and expected to become majority by CY26-end.
Nearline EB capacity is largely allocated through CY27; strategic planning discussions now extend into CY28+, supporting better pricing visibility.
Mgmt raised MT rev growth target to at least 20% from low-to-mid-teens prior, implying stronger durability of AI/hyperscaler storage demand.
Opex leverage is notable: F3Q26 opex 9.5% of sales and F4Q guide implies ~$295mn/8.6% of rev, with dollars expected largely flat over next few qtrs.

reports.title: 2% of CSP CapEx, but Hosts 80% of Their Storage. Pricing Power in Early Innings
reports.headline: TD Cowen raises STX PT to $850 from $500 on stronger HDD pricing power, cloud mix, HAMR ramp, and higher EPS path.
reports.summary: TD Cowen reiterates Buy on STX and lifts PT to $850, arguing HDDs have shifted from a declining/pricing-weak market to a rational cloud-storage duopoly with rising pricing power and expanding TAM. The note says STX delivered a “flawless” qtr, with JunQ EPS guide 25% above Street, GM moving toward ~50% NT and ~60% by CY27, and CY27 EPS upside to high-$30s if ASP growth and exabyte shipments outperform. The core variant view is that HDD pricing power is still under-modeled, helped by tight supply, NAND inflation, cloud mix, and disciplined capacity additions.
important_observations: Cloud mix has risen from ~25% in 2019 to ~65% in 2026E, supporting a higher multiple vs historical low-teens HDD P/E.
TD sees 25x CY27E EPS as reasonable despite pre-GenAI low-teens multiples, implying major re-rating potential.
JunQ EPS guide of $5 was ~25% above Street and supports the prior bull case of $35-40 CY27 EPS.
STX JunQ guide implies nearly 50% GM; TD models ~60% CY27 GM on 9% ASP growth and 25% exabyte shipment growth.
HDD supply is viewed as tighter than NAND, and TD thinks the market is too conservative on HDD OEMs’ ability to pass through more than high-single-digit price increases.
NAND pricing up >200% YTD/this year is viewed as a leading indicator for HDD pricing power; NAND/HDD premium expected to widen from 17x to 25x by YE.
HAMR mix is accelerating: mgmt reiterated most nearline shipments exiting CY26 will be HAMR, up from 40% JunQ per Analyst Day and 25% two qtrs ago.
STX and WDC control ~90% of cloud storage HDD market, while HDDs still store ~80% of cloud exabytes at ~10x lower cost than NAND.

reports.title: The Best Ever Quarter with Substantial Outperformance; Raise PT to $1,617
reports.headline: FICO posted record F2Q26 rev/EBITDA/EPS, raised FY26 guide, and Clear Street raised PT to $1,617 while reiterating Buy.
reports.summary: Clear Street says FICO delivered its best qtr ever, with F2Q26 adj EPS of $12.50 vs $11.01 CS / $10.89 cons and rev of $692M (+39% y/y) vs $670M CS / $630M cons, driven by Scores +60% and mortgage +127%. Mgmt raised FY26 guide across rev, adj NI, and adj EPS while still assuming conservative score vols and no VantageScore share loss. The note argues the regulatory/AI-driven multiple compression is overdone given dominant Scores share, improving Software ARR, strong FCF, and aggressive buybacks, though FHFA/VantageScore, LLPA/securitization mechanics, and mortgage sensitivity remain key watch items.
important_observations: Mortgage is increasingly the earnings fulcrum: +127% y/y and now 72% of B2B Scores / 63% of total Scores rev.
FICO 10T pricing reset to $0.99 per score plus $65 funding fee narrows the upfront price gap vs VantageScore while preserving downstream economics.
Analyst view: bear case is shifting from price competition to performance, regulatory selling rules, and secondary-market acceptance.
Mgmt sees reseller readiness for Mortgage Direct Licensing, with 3 of top 5 signed and remaining 2 in advanced talks, but FHFA approval remains gating item.
Software is re-accelerating beneath the Scores story, with platform ARR +49%, platform rev +54%, SaaS +19%, and platform DBNRR 136%.

reports.title: Strong F2Q bolstered by healthy mortgage score price realization with a favorable competitive outlook
reports.headline: GS reiterates Buy as F2Q beat/raise was driven by mortgage score pricing, 65% EBITDA margin, DLP/10T progress, and higher FY26-28 ests; PT raised to $1,543 from $1,528.
reports.summary: FICO delivered a strong F2Q with rev/EPS materially above GS/cons, led by 127% y/y mortgage origination rev growth from price realization and some volume help, driving 710 bps y/y EBITDA margin expansion to ~65%. GS raises FY26-28 rev/EPS ests on stronger high-margin Scores rev, reiterates Buy, and lifts PT to $1,543 while lowering the target multiple to reflect tri-merge regulatory headline risk. Key debate is whether mortgage score pricing power, DLP go-live, and 10T adoption can sustain growth/margins despite possible FHFA bi-merge changes and legacy software drag.
important_observations: Mortgage origination rev grew 127% y/y and reached 43% of total co rev; GS estimates low-DD volume growth, with most of the increase from price.
DLP is near go-live, with 3 of top 5 mortgage resellers signed and remaining 2 in advanced discussions; top 5 represent ~85-90% of US mortgage score industry by GS estimate.
FICO cut 10T DLP pricing to $0.99/score plus $65 funding fee, matching VantageScore upfront pricing and potentially reducing adoption friction.
Platform ARR growth accel to 49% y/y, or mid-30s ex one-off migrations, with Platform now 44% of software ARR.
FICO repurchased $605mn of shares in F2Q, its largest qtrly buyback, and added a $1.5bn authorization.
new_data: F2Q rev $691.7mn, +38.7% y/y, 12.8% above GS est and above FactSet cons.
Scores rev +59.9% y/y; B2B +72%, B2C +5%; mortgage origination +127%, auto +13%, card/personal loan +6%.
Software rev +7.4% y/y; Software ARR +10.4%, with Platform ARR +48.7% and non-Platform ARR -8.3%.
Adjusted EBITDA $448.5mn, margin 64.8%; adjusted EPS $12.50 vs GS $10.65 and cons $10.89.
FY26 company guide raised: rev to $2.45bn from $2.35bn and EPS to $40.45 from $38.17.
management_insights: Mgmt noted stable-to-improving credit conditions, with modest subprime softness offset by prime/super-prime strength.
Mgmt expects no operational/technical hurdles for DLP resellers; final FHFA/GSE approval is the remaining gating item.
Mgmt sees pent-up lender demand for DLP and performance-based mortgage score pricing.
FY26 guide assumes no VantageScore 4.0 share gains and is indifferent to DLP timing given similar economics under per-score and performance-based models.
Mgmt expects opex to trend modestly higher through FY26 from personnel and marketing spend, but rev growth should outpace expense growth.

reports.headline: FICO and TRU beat, but buy-side expected strength; FICO flowed beat into FY26 guide raise while TRU/EFX remained conservative amid macro/FHFA noise.
reports.summary: Barclays says FICO and TRU delivered strong qtrly beats after EFX, but much of the upside was anticipated and organic guide raises were limited. FICO raised FY26 guide and Barclays lifted FICO EPS ests, staying above guide given conservatism. TRU raised reported guide mostly for Mexico acquisition/FX, but Barclays trimmed FY26 EPS on margin flow-through and intl softness. Key debates remain mortgage upside from price/vols, FHFA/lenders-choice/bi-merge risk, FICO 10T vs VS4 adoption, and whether macro uncertainty keeps credit bureau stocks range-bound.
important_observations: Mortgage remains the main upside in guides, from both FICO effective price near $10 and potential volume recovery vs guide assumptions for declining hard/soft inquiries.

FICO now has 55 lenders in non-Agency 10T early adopter program covering $495B originations and ~$1.62T eligible servicing; Barclays flags this as a counter to FHFA’s 21-lender VS4 pilot.
FICO Direct Lending Program is not live yet, but 3 of top 5 resellers are signed and the other 2 are close; Barclays thinks adoption could be rapid once FHFA approval is final.

reports.title: GSEs now formally accepting VantageScore 4.0 for conforming mortgages on a limited basis
reports.headline: GSEs/FHA begin limited VS4.0 acceptance; GS sees modest bureau upside but bigger tri-merge/policy risk, while FICO mortgage economics look resilient.
reports.summary: GS says Fannie/Freddie and FHA have started accepting VantageScore 4.0 for mortgages, but only via a limited rollout to the 21 largest lenders, est at ~35% of US conforming mortgage vols. FICO 10T is approved but awaits historical performance data expected in coming months/summer. GS views FICO mortgage rev risk as limited due to switching costs, validation history, workflow dependency and pricing flexibility, while EFX/TRU/Experian could see modest VS4.0 upside offset by FHFA scrutiny and potential move away from tri-merge.
important_observations: VS4.0 adoption is real but narrow initially: only 21 largest mortgage lenders can deliver VS4.0-scored conforming loans today.
Top-21 lender cohort is est at ~35% of US conforming mortgage vols, limiting NT addressable impact for bureaus and VantageScore.
FHFA did not provide guardrails for lender score-choice gaming, leaving adverse-selection/model-governance debate unresolved.
FICO 10T delay persists until historical data publication; GSEs expect data in coming months, targeted for summer.
FICO appears positioned to defend economics via bundling/direct licensing pricing despite regulatory push for score competition.
For bureaus, policy risk may outweigh NT VS4.0 upside if FHFA pushes bi-merge or single-score away from tri-merge.
new_data: Fannie/Freddie immediately accepting VS4.0-scored conforming loans after FHFA sign-off on VS4.0 LLPA grids and Selling Guide updates.
FHA confirmed it will permit VS4.0 alongside FICO 10T for FHA-insured mortgages.
GSEs to publish FICO 10T historical data for loans acquired Apr 2013-Sep 2025.
GSEs to release additional VS4.0 historical data spanning Apr 2023-Sep 2025.
FICO Mortgage Direct Licensing Program: 10T priced at $0.99/score + $65 funding fee vs Classic FICO $4.95/score + $33 fee; bureau-distributed pricing unchanged at $10/score for both.
management_insights: FHFA commentary emphasized competition, cost reduction and LT AI disruption rather than detailed gaming safeguards.
FHFA/GSEs indicated FICO 10T historical data availability over coming months, targeted for summer.

estimate_change_details.note: FICO PT history shows Apr 2 PT down from Feb 2; not necessarily changed in this Apr 22 note.
EFX PT history shows Apr 21 PT up from Apr 2; current report reiterates $200 PT.
TRU PT history shows Apr 2 PT down from Feb 13; not necessarily changed in this Apr 22 note.

evidence_quality: Primary regulatory/company data: FHFA, GSE Selling Guide/LLPA updates, FHA confirmation and expected GSE historical-data releases support the implementation facts.
Analyst model work: lender share estimate of ~35% for top-21 cohort and economics implications are GS analysis, not company-reported.
Market/industry data plus analyst inference: FICO pricing details and mortgage workflow stickiness underpin the view that mortgage scoring rev headwinds should be limited.
Policy-risk assessment is mostly analyst inference from FHFA Director comments and lack of stated safeguards; useful but dependent on future rulemaking.
risks_or_watch_items: Timing/content of FICO 10T historical performance data release targeted for summer.
Whether FHFA adds safeguards around lender choice and score gaming.
Expansion pace beyond top-21 lenders and actual lender uptake of VS4.0 vs FICO 10T.
Any FHFA move from tri-merge to bi-merge or single-score, which could pressure bureau mortgage economics.
Headline/regulatory risk could cap FICO valuation even if fundamentals hold.

reports.title: Global Technology: Semiconductors - Memory: Global Memory S/D update and BOM cost analysis
reports.headline: GS expects tighter 2026/27 DRAM, NAND, and HBM S/D, with memory pricing/margins reaching historical highs; reiterates Buy on SEC, Hynix, SNDK, TEL, Ulvac, Disco.
reports.summary: GS raises memory demand/S-D tightness expectations post 4QCY25 results, now modeling one of the largest DRAM/NAND shortages in history in 2026E and a materially tighter HBM market. The key change is stronger server/AI demand, incl ASIC-driven HBM and eSSD demand from Nvidia ICMSP/BlueField architecture, while PC/smartphone demand is cut on weaker shipments and higher memory BOM burden. GS argues demand destruction risk is real but insufficient to break tight S/D, supporting sharp ASP increases, peak-like margins, and Buy recs across memory suppliers and selected equipment beneficiaries.
important_observations: DRAM 2026E shortage now seen as most severe in 15+ yrs, driven by server DDR/LPDDR/HBM/SOCAMM demand, limited cleanroom capacity, and healthy inventories.
Server-related DRAM incl HBM expected to reach 53%/57% of global DRAM demand in 2026E/27E, making server by far the dominant driver.
NAND upside is increasingly tied to eSSD demand; ICMSP/BlueField could add 29EB/79EB NAND demand in 2026/27, or 3%/6% of total NAND demand.
Higher memory costs could pressure PC/smartphone shipments and content, but GS stress case still leaves 2026E DRAM undersupplied by 1.7%.
Conventional memory margins could reach historical highs: 2026E DRAM OPM 71% for SEC and 79% for Hynix; NAND OPM 40%/44%.
HBM mix shifts further toward ASICs, now 33%/36% of 2026E/27E HBM demand vs 28%/34% prior.
new_data: 2026E/27E DRAM S/D revised to 4.9%/2.5% undersupply vs 3.3%/1.1% prior.
2026E/27E NAND S/D revised to 4.2%/2.1% undersupply vs 2.5%/1.2% prior.
2026E/27E HBM TAM raised to $54bn/$75bn from $50bn/$69bn.
2026E/27E HBM S/D revised to 5.1%/4.0% undersupply vs 0.7%/1.6% prior.
2026E/27E global DRAM demand now +25%/+17% y/y vs +23%/+15% prior; NAND demand +22%/+15% vs +19%/+14% prior.
Memory BOM cost expected by 3Q26 at 23% of iPhone BOM, 29% of Galaxy S BOM, and 17% of Dell XPS BOM.
management_insights: SEC guided HBM rev to increase 3x this year; GS expects SEC to focus 1c nm DRAM/TSV capacity and ASIC customers, esp Google TPU.
SEC said HBM4 incl 11.7Gbps SKU begins large-scale customer delivery from Feb 2026.
SanDisk mgmt expects NAND industry to remain increasingly undersupplied in FY3Q and cites incremental 75EB-100EB NAND demand in 2027 from ICMSP.
PC/smartphone OEM comments point to price increases, lower memory configs, and GM pressure from memory inflation.

evidence_quality: Company-reported results/guidance: based on post-4QCY25 earnings, supplier capacity plans, SEC HBM guide, and OEM comments; primary but interpreted through GS model.
Analyst model work: S/D, pricing, margin, HBM TAM, and BOM sensitivity are GS forecasts; useful for variant framing but dependent on capacity/demand assumptions.
Channel checks: customer allocation requests out to 2028 and HBM capacity commentary are check-based; directional but anecdotal unless corroborated.

Supplier capex/capacity additions exceed expectations, especially if cleanroom constraints ease.

NAND recovery could fail to materialize; YMTC roadmap progress could pressure SNDK/NAND pricing.

reports.title: Shifting Ratings Ahead of CQ1 Earnings: RF and Drives Oh My! UG SWKS, QRVO, STX to OW, DG PENG to EW, Reinstate QCOM at UW
reports.companies_covered: PENG, QCOM, QRVO, STX, SWKS, WDC
reports.headline: Barclays rotates toward HDD and RF value, raises HDD ests/PTs, reinstates QCOM UW, and steps aside on PENG margin pressure.
reports.summary: Barclays sees the best semi value in HDD drives and RF after CQ1 earnings setup, arguing HDD pricing has more upside given Flash inflation, disciplined capacity, and STX's 40TB HAMR transition, while SWKS/QRVO become buy-the-cut RF stories after expected AAPL Sep/Dec weakness is digested. QCOM is reinstated UW on handset/memory headwinds and unproven DC AI traction, while PENG is cut to EW as memory pass-through lifts rev but compresses GM and Advanced Computing ramps slower than hoped.
important_observations: HDD industry viewed as structurally tighter, with supply discipline and Flash price inflation supporting higher $/TB and more durable re-rating for STX/WDC.
Barclays raises CY26 HDD rev fcst by ~$4B and CY27 by ~$9B; ASPs, not units, drive most of the growth.
STX 40TB/Mozaic 4+ ramp could improve EB output >30% with similar BOM, supporting GM expansion and narrowing prior cost-structure concerns vs WDC.
RF setup is framed as buy-the-cut: AAPL lower-end SKU timing likely hurts 2H seasonality, but foldable iPhone, IP20 anniversary cycle, and internal modem mix could create later catalysts.
QCOM handset ests sit below Street as memory shortages pressure Android units; Auto/IoT growth not enough yet, and DC inference adoption remains unproven.
PENG top-line benefit from memory pass-through is not viewed as sustainable because GMs fall as input costs rise and Advanced Computing projects slip into FY27.
new_data: SOX outperformed S&P by ~33% YTD through 21-Apr-26 and Software/Internet by ~54%/~34%.
CY26 HDD industry rev raised to ~$29.7B from $25.4B, with units 121.4M vs 109.6M prior and ASPs +33% y/y vs +27% prior.
CY27 HDD industry rev raised to $38.8B from $29.6B, with units 118.9M vs 106.5M prior and ASPs +34% y/y vs +20% prior.
Barclays models STX and WDC rev +39%/+35% and +39%/+32% y/y in CY26/CY27, respectively.
STX Nearline capacity allocated through CY26 and into CY27, with cloud customers discussing CY28 demand projections.
QCOM DC AI customer disclosure remains limited; HUMAIN is only announced customer, with material rev not expected until FY27.
management_insights: STX mgmt expects 50% EB crossover on Nearline HAMR drives in 2H CY26 and Mozaic 4+ volume shipments starting this qtr.
STX/WDC remain resolute on not adding HDD capacity, supporting pricing discipline.
PENG mgmt cited new Advanced Computing logos, but Barclays sees NT contribution as limited and projects pushed into FY27.
QCOM mgmt expected to formalize a more concrete DC plan at Jun analyst day.
QCOM has noted Samsung flagship share floor of at least 75% under a multi-year agreement.

Analyst model work: HDD rev/unit/ASP forecasts, EPS/PT changes, and valuation multiples are Barclays estimates; weight depends on pricing/ASP assumptions.
